title: Richly Aerated Canadian Lemon Puff
description: A crowd-pleasing Canadian dessert featuring unwaxed lemon and Mediterranean-friendly flavors, with a lamination-puff pastry that is both bitter and aerated.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, confectioners sugar, and salt.
Add the cold butter to the bowl and use a pastry blender to work it into the flour mixture until it resembles coarse crumbs.
In a small bowl, whisk together the eggs and water.
Add the egg mixture to the flour mixture and stir until the dough comes together in a ball.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and roll it out into a large rectangle.
Fold the dough in half lengthwise, like a letter, and then fold it in half again, rotating the dough 90 degrees after each fold.
Roll the dough out into a large rectangle again and repeat the folding process two more times.
After the final fold, roll the dough out into a large rectangle and brush the top with olive oil.
Zest the lemons over the top of the dough and sprinkle with a pinch of salt.
Bake the puff in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until golden brown.
